Issued by Emperor Meiji, 30 October 1890. Public domain. Standard English translation published by the Japanese Ministry of Education. Widely reproduced in primary-source collections.
Issued by Emperor Meiji, 4 January 1882. Public domain. English translation widely reproduced in scholarship on Japanese militarism.

Japanese Ministry of Education, 1937. English translation excerpt verified at Asia for Educators, Columbia University: https://afe.easia.columbia.edu/ps/japan/kokutai.pdf. Full translation in de Bary et al., Sources of Japanese Tradition, 2nd ed., vol. 2 (Columbia University Press, 2005).
Imperial Rescript, 8 December 1941. Public domain. English text verified at worldjpn.net: https://worldjpn.net/documents/texts/docs/19411208.O1E.html
Public domain. Available at Wikisource and DC-Cam.

Public domain. Selected Works Vol. 3, verified at marxists.org.

Used as theory background; no copyrighted full text committed.

Public domain party programme. Point 10 directly encodes collective priority over individual activity. Text widely reproduced and stable across editions.

Official legal instrument, public domain. English text verified at GHDI: https://germanhistorydocs.org/en/nazi-germany-1933-1945/the-reichswehr-swears-an-oath-of-allegiance-to-adolf-hitler-on-the-day-of-hindenburgs-death-august-2-1934

Access limitation: authoritative Korean-language text is on DPRK state website (Naenara), accessible only intermittently from outside the DPRK and without independent translation verification. English translation used here is from University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa Korean Studies Center (2024), which is the scholarly standard but cannot be certified against the authoritative original. Kim Jong-un era operational documents (party resolutions, state media) are accessible via KCNA Watch and other monitoring services but present translation-chain verification challenges. This case is limited to constitutional-level primary sources to minimize translation uncertainty.
Public law. Full text at govinfo.gov.
Public domain presidential address. Verified at whatsoproudlywehail.org.
Official English translation, Deutscher Bundestag, 2012 ed. Art. 1(1) and Art. 79(3) (eternity clause) are the key passages for this chain.

First government declaration to the Bundestag, 20 September 1949. Corroborates the constitutional-containment reading by showing the new symbolic order's explicit rejection of the Nazi-era collective-body logic at the moment of the Federal Republic's founding.
Public domain. Verified at avalon.law.yale.edu.
Public domain. Verified at revolution.chnm.org and alphahistory.com.

Delivered 25 December 1991, broadcast on Soviet state television. English translation widely reproduced; passage text requires verification against a scholarly edition.
Signed 21 December 1991 by eleven former Soviet republics. Official governmental document; public domain. English text reproduced in major international law collections.

Report to the CPSU Central Committee Plenary Session, 27 January 1987; published in Pravda and Izvestiia, 28 January 1987. Passage verified at Seventeen Moments in Soviet History (MSU): https://soviethistory.msu.edu/1985/perestroika-and-glasnost/perestroika-and-glasnost-texts/gorbachev-challenges-the-party/
GKChP Appeal to the Soviet People, broadcast 19 August 1991. Passage verified at GlobalSecurity.org: https://www.globalsecurity.org/military/world/war/russia2.htm
Official English translation. Verified at https://www.constituteproject.org/constitution/Switzerland_2014
Public Law 93-148, enacted 7 November 1973. United States federal law; public domain. Full text in US Statutes at Large 87 Stat. 555 and codified at 50 U.S.C. §§ 1541-1548.
Address by President Jimmy Carter, 15 July 1979. Public domain US government document. Archived at the Jimmy Carter Presidential Library and Miller Center, University of Virginia.

Secretary of Defense Caspar W. Weinberger, address to the National Press Club, 28 November 1984. Public domain US government document. Primary source: Office of the Historian, FRUS 1981–1988, vol. I, doc. 219; also archived at PBS FRONTLINE.
Public domain. Clarke (1767) translation at Internet Archive.
